frameworks.name: amortized inference
frameworks.one_line: reuse of prior computations or summary statistics across related probabilistic queries

frameworks.name: sample-based Bayesian cognition
frameworks.one_line: probability distributions over hypotheses approximated by samples

frameworks.name: cognitive load manipulation
frameworks.one_line: load used to distinguish caching of summary statistics from raw samples

core_question: Do people amortize probabilistic inference across related queries by reusing samples or summary statistics?
key_assumptions: people compute probability distributions over hypotheses
inference can be approximated by sampling
related queries share reusable computational substructure
manipulations: structure of a preceding query in a sequential pair
key_findings: answers to a second query depend systematically on the first query's structure
load manipulation distinguishes cached summary statistics from raw sample sets
evidence is more consistent with caching summary statistics
interpretations: the brain approximates probabilistic inference via amortized reuse
summary statistics, not raw samples, are the unit of reuse
